indicators.name: Total postsecondary enrollment
indicators.source_query: National Student Clearinghouse Research Center current term enrollment estimates
indicators.description: Headline signal for whether the demographic cliff is showing up in actual enrollment numbers yet.

current.value: 18.6 million total postsecondary students, up 1.0% YoY
current.note: Total postsecondary enrollment grew 1.0% from spring 2025, which is positive YoY growth and therefore classified as Improving.

history.value: Total postsecondary enrollment reached 18.6 million students, up 1.0% year over year
18.6 million students, up 1.0% year over year (+192,000)
18.6 million total postsecondary students, up 1.0% YoY

indicators.name: High school graduate pipeline
indicators.source: WICHE Knocking at the College Door
indicators.source_query: WICHE Knocking at the College Door high school graduate projections latest
indicators.description: Leading indicator (3-5 yr out) for the size of the future applicant pool.

thresholds.label: Worsening
thresholds.band: Revised projections steeper decline than prior forecast

current.value: 2024 WICHE projection: U.S. high school graduates peak in 2025, then decline 13% through 2041; about 1% fewer graduates in 2037 and roughly 750,000 fewer cumulatively through 2037 versus the 2020 projection.
current.note: The latest Knocking at the College Door update revised the future pipeline lower than the prior forecast, with fewer expected graduates beyond 2025 due mainly to lower-than-expected K-12 enrollments.

history.value: U.S. high school graduates are projected to peak at 3.9 million in 2025, then decline 13% to 3.4 million by 2041; the comparable 2037 projection is about 3.51 million versus about 3.52 million in the 2020 edition.

2024 WICHE 11th edition projects U.S. high school graduates peaking at 3.9 million in 2025, falling to 3.4 million by 2041; through 2037, projections are about 750,000 graduates lower cumulatively than the 2020 edition.

2024 WICHE projection: U.S. high school graduates peak in 2025, then decline 13% through 2041; about 1% fewer graduates in 2037 and roughly 750,000 fewer cumulatively through 2037 versus the 2020 projection.

indicators.name: FAFSA completion rate
indicators.source_query: NCAN FAFSA completion tracker current cycle vs prior year
indicators.description: Near-term leading indicator of next fall's enrollment, especially for access-sensitive institutions.

current.value: 59.1% FAFSA completion rate for the high school class of 2026; 9% more FAFSA completions than the class of 2025 by the June 30 milestone
current.note: NCAN reports the current cycle is ahead of the prior cycle by 9%, which exceeds the >5% threshold for Improving.

history.value: 59.1% FAFSA completion rate for the high school class of 2026; 9% more FAFSAs completed than the class of 2025 by the June 30 milestone
59.1% FAFSA completion rate for the high school class of 2026; class of 2025 was 53.8% through the same date
59.1% FAFSA completion rate for the high school class of 2026; 9% more FAFSA completions than the class of 2025 by the June 30 milestone

trends.title: Alternative Credentials Are Displacing the Degree

indicators.name: Non-degree vs. degree completions
indicators.source_query: National Student Clearinghouse certificate completions growth vs degree completions
indicators.description: Whether certificates/badges are growing faster than degree completions.

thresholds.label: Accelerating
thresholds.band: Non-degree growth outpaces degree growth by > 3x

current.value: Undergraduate certificate earners grew 5.7% year over year, versus bachelor’s degree earners +2.8% and associate degree earners +2.6%.
current.note: Certificate growth is still faster than degree growth, but only about 2x the bachelor’s/associate growth rate, which falls in the 1-3x band.

history.value: Undergraduate certificates +5.7% year over year versus degree completions roughly +2.7% (bachelor's +2.8%, associate +2.6%); about 2.1x faster.
Undergraduate certificates grew 5.7% year over year, versus roughly 2.7% combined growth for associate and bachelor’s degree earners (associate +2.6%, bachelor’s +2.8%).
Undergraduate certificate earners grew 5.7% year over year, versus bachelor’s degree earners +2.8% and associate degree earners +2.6%.

indicators.name: Employer degree-requirement removal rate
indicators.source: LinkedIn Economic Graph / Indeed Hiring Lab
indicators.source_query: job postings dropping degree requirements trend LinkedIn Indeed
indicators.description: Demand-side validation that employers are actually dropping degree requirements.

thresholds.label: Accelerating
thresholds.band: >1 ppt/quarter increase in postings without degree requirement

current.value: 51% of US Indeed job postings did not ask for any formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023.
current.note: The latest Indeed Hiring Lab update shows degree requirements rising rather than postings without degree requirements increasing, so the removal trend is flat or reversing.

history.value: 51% of Indeed U.S. job postings listed no formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023.
51% of US Indeed job postings listed no formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023
51% of US Indeed job postings did not ask for any formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023.

indicators.name: Institutional AI/credential adoption
indicators.source_query: EDUCAUSE AI landscape survey latest results credential adoption
indicators.description: Whether institutions are building AI-related credentials and adapting curricula.

thresholds.label: Accelerating
thresholds.band: Majority of institutions report new AI-related credentials

current.value: 54% of respondents reported teaching-and-learning AI focus on curriculum design; coursework was 65%.
current.note: The latest EDUCAUSE AI Landscape Study shows a majority of institutions are already adapting curriculum-related activity around AI, which fits the Accelerating band.

history.value: 54% of responding institutions reported already using AI to support curriculum design
54% reported curriculum design as a teaching-and-learning AI focus; coursework was 65%; no separate new AI-credential percentage was reported in the public key findings.
54% of respondents reported teaching-and-learning AI focus on curriculum design; coursework was 65%.

indicators.name: Enterprises with AI in production
indicators.source: McKinsey State of AI annual survey
indicators.source_query: McKinsey State of AI survey enterprises AI in production latest
indicators.description: Gap between AI experimentation and real production deployment.

thresholds.label: Accelerating
thresholds.band: >10 ppt YoY increase in production deployment

current.value: 31% scaling and 7% fully scaled among AI-using organizations; approximately one-third of respondents have begun scaling AI programs
current.note: McKinsey's latest State of AI survey provides a deployment snapshot but not a directly comparable YoY production-deployment rate, so the threshold band cannot be classified confidently.

history.value: Latest McKinsey State of AI survey reports about one-third of all respondents have begun scaling AI programs; among AI-using organizations, 31% are scaling and 7% are fully scaled.
Approximately one-third of respondents said their organizations are scaling AI programs across their organizations; 88% reported regular AI use in at least one business function.
31% scaling and 7% fully scaled among AI-using organizations; approximately one-third of respondents have begun scaling AI programs

indicators.name: AI-related job postings share
indicators.source: LinkedIn Economic Graph / Indeed Hiring Lab
indicators.source_query: AI job postings share of total hiring trend latest
indicators.description: Real-time labor-market demand signal for AI skills.

current.value: LinkedIn reports U.S. national AI job-post share grew +24.7% YoY; the most recent exact Indeed share found was 4.2% at end-2025.
current.note: The latest recent source gives a YoY growth rate but not the underlying share or month-to-month percentage-point change needed to classify against the dashboard bands.

history.value: US AI-related job postings share was 5.7% of postings, 7-day trailing average
Last exact total-postings share found was Indeed AI Tracker at 4.2%; newer July 2026 Indeed update reports Q1 2026 AI-touched job-title breadth, not total posting share.
LinkedIn reports U.S. national AI job-post share grew +24.7% YoY; the most recent exact Indeed share found was 4.2% at end-2025.

indicators.name: Hyperscaler AI capex
indicators.source: Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta quarterly earnings
indicators.source_query: Microsoft Google Amazon Meta AI capex guidance latest quarter
indicators.description: Whether infrastructure investment still supports the demand narrative.

current.value: Combined 2026 hyperscaler capex guidance increased to about $695-$725B: Microsoft ~$190B, Amazon ~$200B, Google $180-$190B, Meta $125-$145B.
current.note: The latest Q1 earnings updates raised combined guidance versus the prior quarter, with Google and Meta increasing ranges, Microsoft giving a higher calendar-year figure, and Amazon holding at $200B.

history.value: Combined 2026 capex plans for Microsoft, Alphabet/Google, Amazon and Meta are about $725B; Q1 combined capex hit roughly $130B.
Combined 2026 capex guidance for Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft rose to about $695-$725B, versus $640B+ in January 2026 guidance.
Combined 2026 hyperscaler capex guidance increased to about $695-$725B: Microsoft ~$190B, Amazon ~$200B, Google $180-$190B, Meta $125-$145B.

indicators.name: Tech layoffs volume
indicators.source_query: layoffs.fyi tech layoffs monthly total latest
indicators.description: Direct measure of workforce contraction in the sector.

current.note: Layoffs.fyi's latest 2026 event table shows June 2026 as the newest month, and summing reported employee counts gives 4,452, below the 5,000/month improving threshold.

history.value: 4,452 reported layoffs
7,252 reported layoffs month-to-date
4,452 reported layoffs/month

indicators.name: Tech/information sector unemployment rate
indicators.source_query: BLS unemployment rate information sector professional business services latest
indicators.description: Whether layoffs are translating into sustained unemployment or fast re-absorption.

current.value: Information unemployment rate: 5.1%; Professional and business services: 3.8%
current.note: The Information rate rose from 3.6% in March 2026 to 5.1% in June 2026, a +1.5 percentage-point increase that exceeds the worsening threshold, while professional/business services declined from 4.2% to 3.8%.

history.value: Information unemployment rate: 5.1%; Professional and business services: 3.8%
Information unemployment rate 5.1%; Professional and business services 3.8%
Information unemployment rate: 5.1%; Professional and business services: 3.8%

indicators.name: AI regulation activity
indicators.source_query: IAPP NCSL AI legislation tracker enacted laws latest
indicators.description: Regulatory overhead that could shape hiring, compliance costs, and product velocity.

thresholds.label: Accelerating
thresholds.band: New enacted state/federal AI laws this quarter

current.value: IAPP reported 11 states had passed chatbot AI laws regulating consumer-facing AI systems, with Hawaii awaiting the governor’s signature.
current.note: The latest IAPP update shows multiple state AI laws already passed, so regulatory activity is in the enacted-laws band rather than just bills advancing.

history.value: 11 states had passed chatbot AI laws regulating consumer-facing AI systems; Hawaii had a similar bill awaiting the governor's signature.
Illinois SB 315, the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, was signed into law on July 6, 2026.
IAPP reported 11 states had passed chatbot AI laws regulating consumer-facing AI systems, with Hawaii awaiting the governor’s signature.

indicators.name: Share of postings dropping degree requirements
indicators.source: LinkedIn Economic Graph / Indeed Hiring Lab
indicators.source_query: job postings dropping degree requirements trend latest
indicators.description: Labor-market side view that cross-validates the Higher Ed credential-substitution trend.

current.value: 51% of US Indeed job postings did not ask for any formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023
current.note: Classified Stalling because Indeed reports degree requirements have risen since early 2024, implying postings dropping or omitting degree requirements are flat or reversing rather than increasing.

history.value: 51% of US Indeed postings did not ask for any formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor’s degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023.
51% of Indeed job postings listed no formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher
51% of US Indeed job postings did not ask for any formal education requirement; 19.3% required a bachelor's degree or higher, up from 16.6% in November 2023

indicators.name: Skills-based hiring adoption rate
indicators.source_query: LinkedIn Talent Solutions skills-based hiring adoption report latest
indicators.description: Whether employers report actually changing hiring practice, not just posting language.

current.value: No actual skills-based hiring adoption-rate figure found; the latest LinkedIn Future of Recruiting 2025 report instead reports 93% of TA pros say accurately assessing skills is crucial and 26% of paid job posts did not require a degree in 2023.
current.note: The latest LinkedIn Talent Solutions annual report does not provide a YoY measure of employers actually changing hiring practices, so it cannot be classified against the percentage-point growth thresholds.

history.value: No employer-reported skills-based hiring adoption-rate YoY figure found; latest LinkedIn report only provides related metrics such as 93% saying accurate skills assessment is crucial, +12% quality-hire association for skills-based searches, and 26% of paid job posts without degree requirements in 2023.
history.as_of: 2025 report; platform data through Oct 2024 and job-posting metric for 2023

history.value: No comparable employer-reported skills-based hiring adoption rate found in the latest LinkedIn Talent Solutions annual report

No actual skills-based hiring adoption-rate figure found; the latest LinkedIn Future of Recruiting 2025 report instead reports 93% of TA pros say accurately assessing skills is crucial and 26% of paid job posts did not require a degree in 2023.

indicators.name: WEF net displacement forecast
indicators.source: World Economic Forum Future of Jobs Report
indicators.source_query: World Economic Forum Future of Jobs Report net displacement forecast latest
indicators.description: Structural, long-horizon signal on net job creation vs. displacement from automation/AI.

current.value: Net +78 million jobs by 2030: 170 million roles created and 92 million displaced, equal to +7% of current formal employment
current.note: The latest Future of Jobs Report forecast moved from the prior 2023 net loss estimate toward clear net job creation, so it is classified as improving.

history.value: 170 million roles created and 92 million displaced by 2030; net +78 million jobs, with 22% structural labour-market churn
170 million jobs projected to be created and 92 million displaced, for a net increase of 78 million jobs by 2030
Net +78 million jobs by 2030: 170 million roles created and 92 million displaced, equal to +7% of current formal employment

trends.title: Hybrid Work Norm Solidifies, RTO Mandates Plateau

indicators.name: Return-to-office mandate tracker
indicators.source_query: Flex Index return to office mandates trend latest
indicators.description: Whether major employers are still tightening in-office requirements or have plateaued.

current.value: 34% of U.S. firms require full-time office presence, up 2 percentage points from last year; 29% of Fortune 100 firms require full-time office and 45% require 4 or 5 days in office.
current.note: The latest Flex Index update still shows a net increase in strict in-office requirements, especially among large employers, so this is classified as tightening rather than plateaued.

history.value: Flex Index reports 34% of U.S. firms require full-time office presence, up 2 percentage points year over year; among Fortune 100 firms, 45% require 4–5 days in office.
WFH Research reports 26.0% of full paid working days were work-from-home days in June 2026, with employer plans hovering around 1.3–1.5 WFH days per week since mid-2022.
34% of U.S. firms require full-time office presence, up 2 percentage points from last year; 29% of Fortune 100 firms require full-time office and 45% require 4 or 5 days in office.

indicators.name: Remote job postings share
indicators.source_query: Ladders FlexJobs remote job postings share trend latest
indicators.description: Direct measure of remote-eligible hiring supply.

current.value: Remote postings were 6.3% of high-paying job listings, down from 7.4% in Q1 2025
current.note: The latest Ladders share fell by 1.1 percentage points quarter over quarter, just over the threshold for Declining.

history.value: Latest FlexJobs report found remote job postings up 20% QoQ, but it did not publish a remote-postings share; latest Ladders share found was 6.3% in Q2 2025, down from 7.4% in Q1 2025.
history.as_of: Q1 2026 for FlexJobs volume; Q2 2025 for latest Ladders share

history.value: Remote job postings increased 22% quarter over quarter in the FlexJobs database
Remote postings were 6.3% of high-paying job listings, down from 7.4% in Q1 2025

current.note: The latest BLS JOLTS overall quits rate is below 2.0%, placing it in the Easing band for labor market confidence and mobility.
